mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in the unemployment rate signals accelerating labor market tightening, which forces immediate equity repricing upward over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 45% of instances when the unemployment rate drops by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and yields negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines are followed by positive 20-day returns in >50% of cases during late-cycle phases, or predictive power vanishes when GDP growth exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers immediate market anticipation of tighter monetary policy, causing sharp negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of months where CPI inflation accelerates past the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with expanding real GDP confirms synchronized economic growth, generating strong positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of quarters, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales indicate weakening consumer spending power, which directly reduces corporate revenue forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines are followed by positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances during deleveraging periods, or the signal fails to predict returns when housing starts are rising.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect strengthening supply-side momentum, which immediately boosts equity valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 40% of months following an industrial production increase, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 3-year window.
